---
title: "Graphviz & DBML"
---

Graphviz [@gansner2000graphviz] has been the reference system for
automatic graph layout for over two decades, and its `dot` language is
correspondingly the best-known graph notation in existence — which makes
it a natural fit for agent-authored figures. Quarto supports dot in
executable code cells; livefigures adds what that leaves out:
file-referenced `.dot`/`.gv` figures with caching and full figure
semantics (@fig-deps). DBML (@fig-schema) brings the same treatment to
database schemas: tables, types, and relationships in a compact DSL that
renders to an entity-relationship diagram. Both render locally — no
network involved. Click to zoom.

```{.dot #fig-deps fig-cap="A dependency graph, inline dot"}
digraph pipeline {
  rankdir=LR;
  node [shape=box, style=rounded];
  filter -> cache -> renderer -> svg;
  filter -> backends;
}
```

```{.dbml #fig-schema fig-cap="A schema, inline DBML"}
Table authors {
  id int [pk]
  name varchar
}

Table figures {
  id int [pk]
  author_id int [ref: > authors.id]
  format varchar
}
```
